title: Encyclia hodgeana
taxonomy: Encyclia hodgeana is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central America. The classification of Encyclia hodgeana is further divided into its genus, which is a group of orchids characterized by their showy flowers and slender stems. The genus Encyclia is itself part of the subtribe Cymbidiinae within the tribe Cymbidieae. This orchid species is often found growing in shaded, humid environments.

intro: Encyclia hodgeana, commonly known as the Hodge's Encyclia, is a species of terrestrial orchid native to Central America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds and infections. The bioactive constituents of Encyclia hodgeana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Encyclia hodgeana is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
